anti_patterns.name: bare_except
anti_patterns.description: Bare 'except:' catches everything including SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt. Always specify the exception type.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect except: without an exception class.
anti_patterns.fix: Use 'except Exception as e:' at minimum. Prefer catching specific exceptions.

anti_patterns.name: star_import
anti_patterns.description: Wildcard imports (from module import *) pollute the namespace and make it unclear where names come from.
anti_patterns.fix: Import specific names: from module import ClassA, function_b

anti_patterns.name: isinstance_chain
anti_patterns.description: Long chains of isinstance checks suggest missing polymorphism or pattern matching (match/case in 3.10+).
anti_patterns.detection: Detect 3+ consecutive isinstance checks in if/elif chains.
anti_patterns.fix: Use match/case (Python 3.10+), dictionary dispatch, or polymorphism.

anti_patterns.name: global_variable
anti_patterns.description: Global mutable state makes code hard to test and reason about. Pass state explicitly or use dependency injection.
anti_patterns.fix: Pass the value as a function parameter. Use a class to encapsulate state.

anti_patterns.name: string_format_percent
anti_patterns.description: % string formatting is outdated. Use f-strings (3.6+) or str.format() for clarity and safety.

anti_patterns.name: assert_in_production
anti_patterns.description: assert statements are stripped when Python runs with -O flag. Never use assert for input validation or security checks.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect assert used for input validation in non-test code.
anti_patterns.fix: Use if/raise ValueError for input validation. Keep assert for internal invariants only.

anti_patterns.name: mutable_class_variable
anti_patterns.description: Mutable class-level variables (list, dict, set) are shared across all instances. A mutation on one instance affects all others.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect class-level assignments to [], {}, or set().
anti_patterns.fix: Move mutable defaults into __init__: self.items = [] instead of items = [] at class level.

anti_patterns.name: len_comparison
anti_patterns.description: Comparing len(x) to 0 is non-Pythonic. Empty containers are falsy, non-empty are truthy.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect if len(x) == 0, if len(x) > 0, if len(x) != 0.
anti_patterns.fix: Use 'if not x:' instead of 'if len(x) == 0:' and 'if x:' instead of 'if len(x) > 0:'.

anti_patterns.name: type_comparison
anti_patterns.description: Using type(x) == or type(x) is for type checks ignores inheritance. Use isinstance() instead.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect type(x) == Type or type(x) is Type patterns.
anti_patterns.fix: Replace type(x) == int with isinstance(x, int). Supports tuples: isinstance(x, (int, float)).

anti_patterns.name: manual_dict_check
anti_patterns.description: Checking if key exists then accessing dict[key] is redundant. Use dict.get() or try/except KeyError.
anti_patterns.detection: Detect if key in dict: value = dict[key] patterns.
anti_patterns.fix: Use value = d.get(key, default) or try: value = d[key] except KeyError: ...
